questions.question: What are Gottman's Four Horsemen of relationship conflict?

options.text: Love, respect, kindness, patience
Criticism (character attack), Contempt (disgust/superiority), Defensiveness (denying responsibility), Stonewalling (complete withdrawal)
Happiness, sadness, anger, fear
Communication, trust, intimacy, commitment

questions.explanation: The Four Horsemen are destructive conflict patterns: (1) Criticism = attacking partner's character ('You're so [trait]') vs. addressing behavior; (2) Contempt = expressions of disgust, superiority, disdain (most corrosive predictor); (3) Defensiveness = denying responsibility, making excuses, counter-attacking; (4) Stonewalling = complete withdrawal, refusing to engage. Research shows contempt is strongest predictor of relationship dissolution. These are observational correlates of distress.

questions.question: What is the research controversy around Gottman's divorce prediction accuracy claims?

options.text: The '83-91% prediction accuracy' is universally accepted and proven
Methodological criticisms include overfitting to training data, lack of independent replication showing substantially lower accuracy, and base rate issues
Gottman never claimed prediction accuracy
The patterns have no relationship to outcomes

questions.explanation: The '83-91% prediction accuracy' claim is methodologically contested: (1) Overfitting to training data (prediction models developed and tested on SAME sample without independent validation), (2) Independent researchers have NOT replicated the high accuracy, (3) Base rate issues (if 50% divorce, predicting 'divorce' for all high-horsemen couples inflates accuracy). The patterns ARE associated with distress (valuable observational finding), but prediction accuracy is oversold. Use as warning signs to address, not proven prophecy.

questions.question: What are the antidotes to the Four Horsemen?

options.text: Ignore the problem completely
Gentle startup (vs. criticism), build culture of appreciation (vs. contempt), accept responsibility (vs. defensiveness), physiological self-soothing (vs. stonewalling)
Attack back harder
There are no antidotes

questions.explanation: Antidotes: (1) Gentle startup: specific behavior + 'I' statement vs. character attack; (2) Culture of appreciation: daily appreciations, notice positive, express admiration (can't feel contempt while actively appreciating); (3) Accept responsibility: even partial ('You're right about [X], I'm sorry') breaks attack-defend cycle; (4) Physiological self-soothing: '20-minute break when flooded' with commitment to return (NOT stonewalling if you return). These counter each horseman effectively.

questions.question: When do the Four Horsemen indicate abuse rather than conflict skills deficits?

options.text: Four Horsemen always indicate abuse
When contempt is one-directional and persistent, patterns include fear of partner, control beyond conflict (monitoring/isolation/financial control), or physical violence/threats
Four Horsemen and abuse are completely unrelated
Only if all four horsemen appear simultaneously

questions.explanation: Safety distinction: Horsemen as skills deficit = bi-directional (both partners engage during stress), neither feels afraid, can de-escalate/repair/reconnect, relationship feels safe between conflicts. Abuse indicators = one-directional persistent contempt (one always expresses disgust/superiority, other always target), fear of partner's reactions, control beyond conflict (monitoring, isolation, financial control), degradation/humiliation, physical violence/threats. If fear + control + one-directional contempt exist, this is abuse requiring safety intervention (National DV Hotline: 1-800-799-7233), not communication coaching.

questions.question: Which statement accurately reflects current evidence on the Four Horsemen?

options.text: Showing any horsemen once guarantees divorce
Horsemen are strongly associated with relationship distress, with contempt most corrosive; prediction accuracy claims are oversold; useful as self-awareness tool for behavior change
Horsemen have no relationship to outcomes
Only contempt matters — other horsemen are irrelevant

questions.explanation: Evidence-accurate summary: Four Horsemen ARE strongly associated with relationship distress and dissolution (observational research replicated across studies); contempt is most corrosive predictor; BUT 'prediction accuracy' percentages are methodologically contested (overfitting, lack of independent replication). Most couples show some horsemen occasionally under stress — what matters: frequency, intensity, ability to repair. Use patterns as self-awareness tool ('I criticize during conflict; I'll practice gentle startup'), not as doom prophecy ('We showed horsemen last fight; we're definitely divorcing').

title: The Four Horsemen (with Gottman Caveats) - Quiz
